output:
Successfully added three async methods to StorageHandler in src/common/storage_handler.py:

1. `get_message_media_understanding(message_id, attachment_index, model)` — selects a single row by the composite PK (message_id, attachment_index, model). Returns dict or None.

2. `get_message_media_understanding_by_hash(content_hash, model=None, media_kind=None)` — selects where content_hash matches, ordered by created_at DESC (newest first), returns first row or None. Optional model/media_kind filters.

3. `upsert_message_media_understanding(row)` — upserts using Supabase's native .upsert() with on_conflict='message_id,attachment_index,model'. No guild_gating — the table intentionally has no guild_id column. understanding is normalized via existing _as_json_object helper.

All three methods are async, follow existing codebase patterns (asyncio.to_thread, .eq/.order/.limit chains, _clean_payload). Syntax validated. Module imports cleanly. All 23 existing tests continue to pass with zero regressions.
commands_run: python -c "import inspect; ... verify all three methods are coroutine functions"

task_updates.executor_notes: Added three async methods to StorageHandler class in src/common/storage_handler.py (lines 2755–2850): get_message_media_understanding (select by PK: message_id, attachment_index, model), get_message_media_understanding_by_hash (select by content_hash, ordered by created_at desc, optional model/media_kind filters), upsert_message_media_understanding (upsert via supabase .upsert() with on_conflict='message_id,attachment_index,model'). sense_check_acknowledgments.detail: All three methods are async (verified via inspect.iscoroutinefunction). upsert_message_media_understanding has NO guild_id column in payload and no guild filtering — confirms skip guild gating. get_message_media_understanding_by_hash uses .order('created_at', desc=True) at line 2800. understanding normalized via self._as_json_object() matching existing pattern.
